attacking_style: Long-range breakaway specialist willing to commit to extended efforts (62km documented), but lacks finishing power to sustain through multiple climbs or late-race gradients.
team_tactics: Operates independently in early breakaways, appearing to target aggression/breakaway classifications rather than stage wins. Limited evidence of coordinated team support.
terrain_preference: Flat to rolling terrain in breakaways; struggles on sustained climbs where peloton applies organized pressure. Heat conditions notably weaken performance.
vulnerability: Endurance ceiling exposed on multi-climb stages after long breakaway efforts. Crash-prone when fatigued. Cannot defend breakaway advantage when peloton commits climbing tempo.
key_patterns: Commits to long-distance breakaways exceeding 60km, prioritizing visibility over realistic win chances.
Folds on climbs following extended breakaway efforts, showing poor recovery between sustained efforts.
Targeted aggression/breakaway classifications rather than stage results in Tour of Oman.
Vulnerable in hot conditions, struggling to maintain output on penultimate climbs after early efforts.
form_trajectory: negative: both documented race appearances ended prematurely due to either crash or collapse under fatigue/heat stress
